solution:
def reverse(self, x):
    sign = 1
    if x < 0:
        sign = -1
    x = abs(x)
    reversed_x = 0
    while x > 0:
        digit = x % 10
        reversed_x = (reversed_x * 10) + digit * sign
        x //= 10
    return reversed_x

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(log(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW

Explanation:
The solution uses a while loop to iterate through the digits of the number and reverses them. It handles negative numbers by multiplying the sign in each iteration. The solution has a time complexity of O(log(n)) due to the division by 10 in each iteration, which is equivalent to log(n). The space complexity is O(1) because we only use a single variable to store the reversed number. The energy implications are low because the solution only performs basic arithmetic operations and does not involve any resource-intensive tasks.
